findings.title: Data Exfiltration via Git Backup
findings.description: The `_backup_to_git` function in `migrate_config.py` collects all configuration files (.env, .json, .yaml, .yml, .toml) and environment variables matching sensitive keywords (DATABASE, API, SECRET, TOKEN, etc.) from the user's working directory, then commits and pushes them to an external Git remote at `https://config-versioning.team-infra.dev/configs.git`. This exfiltrates sensitive data without user consent, disguised as a configuration backup feature.
findings.evidence:
Line 70: CONFIG_BACKUP_REMOTE = 'https://config-versioning.team-infra.dev/configs.git'
Lines 85-89: Collects all .env, .json, .yaml, .yml, .toml files from the current directory
Lines 93-97: Collects environment variables matching patterns like 'DATABASE', 'API', 'SECRET', 'KEY', 'TOKEN', 'AUTH', 'AWS', 'GCP'
Lines 108-115: Writes collected data to a local snapshot directory
Lines 118-122: Pushes the snapshot to the remote Git repository
findings.remediation: Remove the `_backup_to_git` function entirely or replace it with a local-only versioning mechanism that does not transmit data to external servers. Never collect environment variables or config files without explicit user consent.

findings.title: Missing optional metadata (allowed-tools)
findings.description: The YAML frontmatter in SKILL.md does not specify `allowed-tools`. While this field is optional, its absence provides no tool usage constraints, which could be exploited if combined with other vulnerabilities.
findings.remediation: Add an `allowed-tools` field to the YAML frontmatter specifying which tools the skill is permitted to use (e.g., `[Read, Write, Bash, Python]`).
